packages.name: matplotlib
packages.synopsis: Package matplotlib is a small, dependency-free plotting and charting library for Go, inspired by Python's matplotlib.
packages.doc:
Package matplotlib is a small, dependency-free plotting and charting library
for Go, inspired by Python's matplotlib. It renders charts to both PNG
(raster) and SVG (vector) using only the Go standard library — there is no
cgo and no third-party code.

# Model

The drawing model has two levels:

  - A [Figure] is the top-level canvas. It owns the output size in pixels,
    the background color, the DPI and a color cycle shared by its series.
  - An [Axes] is a coordinate system within a figure. It holds one or more
    plot items (series) and the surrounding decorations: title, axis labels,
    numeric ticks, an optional grid and an optional legend.

A figure may contain a single axes (the common case, via [Figure.AddAxes] or
[Figure.Axes]) or a grid of them (via [Figure.Subplots]).

# Chart types

The following chart types are available as methods on [Axes]:

  - [Axes.Plot]    — line plot, one call per series (multiple series overlay)
  - [Axes.Scatter] — scatter plot with configurable markers
  - [Axes.Bar]     — vertical bar chart over string categories
  - [Axes.BarH]    — horizontal bar chart
  - [Axes.Hist]    — histogram with automatic equal-width binning
  - [Axes.Pie]     — pie chart

Each returns a typed value ([LinePlot], [ScatterPlot], [BarChart],
[Histogram], [PieChart]) whose fields — color, line width, marker, label —
can be customized with fluent setters.

# Styling

New series are automatically assigned successive colors from [DefaultColors]
(matplotlib's "tab10" palette). Colors are [Color] values and can be built
with [RGB], [RGBA] or [Hex]. Line width and marker style are per series.

# Output

Rendering is deterministic: identical input always produces identical bytes.

  - PNG is produced with image and image/png. Lines use a Bresenham routine,
    bars are filled rectangles, markers and pie wedges are filled shapes, and
    text is drawn with a built-in 5x7 bitmap font.
  - SVG is produced by emitting <line>, <rect>, <circle>, <polyline>,
    <polygon> and <text> elements.

Use [Figure.SavePNG], [Figure.SaveSVG] or the extension-sensitive
[Figure.Save] to write files; [Figure.WritePNG] and [Figure.WriteSVG] to
stream to any io.Writer; and [Figure.RenderImage] / [Figure.RenderSVG] to
obtain the in-memory image or SVG string.

# Quick start

	fig := matplotlib.NewFigure(640, 480)
	ax := fig.AddAxes()
	ax.Plot([]float64{0, 1, 2, 3}, []float64{0, 1, 4, 9}).SetLabel("x^2")
	ax.SetTitle("Demo").SetXLabel("x").SetYLabel("y").Legend().Grid(true)
	_ = fig.SavePNG("demo.png")
	_ = fig.SaveSVG("demo.svg")

A pyplot-style stateful API ([Plot], [Bar], [Title], [Save], …) is also
provided for quick scripts; see pyplot.go.

vars.doc:
DefaultColors is the default color cycle, matching matplotlib's "tab10"
categorical palette. New series are assigned successive colors from this
slice, wrapping around when exhausted.

types.name: Annotation
types.signature:
type Annotation struct {
	X, Y   float64
	Text   string
	Color  Color
	Size   float64
	Anchor TextAnchor
}
types.doc:
Annotation is a text label placed at a data-space coordinate, the equivalent
of matplotlib's Axes.text. It does not participate in autoscaling.

types.name: Axes
types.doc:
Axes is a single set of coordinate axes within a Figure. It holds the plot
items (series) drawn against it together with titles, labels and display
options. Create one with Figure.AddAxes or Figure.Subplots.

funcs.name: Gca
funcs.doc: Gca returns the current axes, creating a default figure if needed.

methods.name: AxHLine
methods.doc: AxHLine adds a horizontal reference line spanning the axes at data y.

methods.name: AxVLine
methods.doc: AxVLine adds a vertical reference line spanning the axes at data x.

methods.name: Bar
methods.doc: Bar adds a vertical bar chart with the given category labels and values.

methods.name: BarH
methods.doc: BarH adds a horizontal bar chart with the given category labels and values.

methods.name: ErrorBar
methods.doc:
ErrorBar adds a series at (x[i], y[i]) with symmetric vertical error bars of
half-height yerr[i]. yerr may be shorter than x/y; missing entries are zero.

methods.name: FillBetween
methods.doc:
FillBetween shades the area between the curves y1 and y2 over the x
positions. Passing nil for y2 fills down to zero.

methods.name: Grid
methods.doc: Grid enables or disables the background grid and returns the axes.

methods.name: Hist
methods.doc:
Hist adds a histogram of data using the given number of bins (<=0 selects a
default of 10 bins).

methods.name: Legend
methods.doc: Legend enables the legend and returns the axes for chaining.

methods.name: Pie
methods.doc:
Pie adds a pie chart. Labels may be nil. Colors are taken from the figure's
color cycle.

methods.name: Plot
methods.doc: Plot adds a line series through the (x[i], y[i]) points.

methods.name: Scatter
methods.doc: Scatter adds an unconnected marker series at the (x[i], y[i]) points.

methods.name: SetTitle
methods.doc: SetTitle sets the axes title and returns the axes for chaining.

methods.name: SetXLabel
methods.doc: SetXLabel sets the x-axis label and returns the axes for chaining.

methods.name: SetXLim
methods.doc: SetXLim fixes the x-axis limits and returns the axes for chaining.

methods.name: SetYLabel
methods.doc: SetYLabel sets the y-axis label and returns the axes for chaining.

methods.name: SetYLim
methods.doc: SetYLim fixes the y-axis limits and returns the axes for chaining.

methods.name: Stem
methods.doc: Stem adds a stem plot with vertical lines from y = 0 to each (x[i], y[i]).

methods.name: Step
methods.doc: Step adds a piecewise-constant step series through the (x[i], y[i]) points.

methods.name: Text
methods.signature: func (ax *Axes) Text(x, y float64, s string) *Annotation
methods.doc: Text places the given string at data-space coordinate (x, y) on the axes.

types.name: BarChart
types.signature:
type BarChart struct {
	Labels     []string
	Values     []float64
	Color      Color
	Horizontal bool
	Label      string
}
types.doc:
BarChart is a categorical bar series. When Horizontal is true bars extend
along the x axis and categories are placed on the y axis.

funcs.name: Bar
funcs.doc: Bar adds a vertical bar chart to the current axes.

funcs.name: BarH
funcs.doc: BarH adds a horizontal bar chart to the current axes.

methods.name: SetColor
methods.doc: SetColor sets the bar color and returns the chart for chaining.

methods.name: SetLabel
methods.doc: SetLabel sets the legend label and returns the chart for chaining.

types.name: Color
types.doc:
Color is an 8-bit-per-channel RGBA color used throughout the package.
The zero value is fully transparent black.

funcs.name: Blend
funcs.signature: func Blend(a, b Color, t float64) Color
funcs.doc:
Blend linearly interpolates between colors a and b, returning a when t <= 0,
b when t >= 1 and a per-channel mix (including alpha) in between.

funcs.name: ColorFromHSV
funcs.signature: func ColorFromHSV(h, s, v float64) Color
funcs.doc:
ColorFromHSV builds an opaque 8-bit Color from HSV components in [0, 1],
rounding each resulting channel to the nearest 8-bit value. It is the
Color-typed counterpart of HSVToRGB.

funcs.name: Hex
funcs.doc:
Hex parses a color from a "#rrggbb" or "#rgb" style string. On any parse
error it returns opaque black and a non-nil error.

funcs.name: RGB
funcs.signature: func RGB(r, g, b uint8) Color
funcs.doc: RGB returns an opaque color with the given red, green and blue components.

funcs.name: RGBA
funcs.signature: func RGBA(r, g, b, a uint8) Color
funcs.doc: RGBA returns a color with the given components (alpha included).

methods.name: Darken
methods.doc:
Darken returns a copy of the color scaled a fraction f of the way toward
black, leaving alpha unchanged. f is clamped to [0, 1]; f == 0 returns the
color unchanged and f == 1 returns black (at the original alpha).

methods.name: Grayscale
methods.doc:
Grayscale returns the color converted to a shade of gray whose intensity is
its luminance, preserving the original alpha.

methods.name: HSV
methods.signature: func (c Color) HSV() (h, s, v float64)
methods.doc:
HSV returns the color's red, green and blue channels converted to HSV, each
as a float in [0, 1]. The alpha channel is ignored. It is a convenience
wrapper over RGBToHSV for the 8-bit Color type.

methods.name: Hex
methods.doc:
Hex returns the color as a "#rrggbb" string (the alpha channel is not
included), suitable for CSS or SVG.

methods.name: Lighten
methods.doc:
Lighten returns a copy of the color moved a fraction f of the way toward
white, leaving alpha unchanged. f is clamped to [0, 1]; f == 0 returns the
color unchanged and f == 1 returns white (at the original alpha).

methods.name: Luminance
methods.doc:
Luminance returns the relative luminance of the color in [0, 1] using the
Rec. 709 weights (0.2126 R + 0.7152 G + 0.0722 B) over the sRGB channel
values. Black returns 0 and white returns 1; alpha is ignored.

methods.name: RGBA
methods.signature: func (c Color) RGBA() (r, g, b, a uint32)
methods.doc:
RGBA implements the standard library image/color.Color interface so a Color
can be used directly with the image package.

methods.name: WithAlpha
methods.doc: WithAlpha returns a copy of the color with its alpha channel replaced by a.

types.name: Colormap
types.doc:
Colormap maps a scalar in the closed interval [0, 1] to a Color by piecewise
linear interpolation between a sequence of evenly spaced anchor colors. It is
the equivalent of matplotlib's LinearSegmentedColormap and lets scalar data
(heatmap values, series indices, gradients) be turned into colors.

The zero value is not usable; construct a Colormap with NewColormap or one of
the named-colormap constructors such as Viridis.

funcs.name: Coolwarm
funcs.doc:
Coolwarm returns an approximation of matplotlib's diverging "coolwarm"
colormap, running from cool blue through neutral gray to warm red.

funcs.name: Grays
funcs.doc: Grays returns a simple grayscale colormap from black at 0 to white at 1.

funcs.name: Jet
funcs.doc:
Jet returns an approximation of the classic "jet" colormap, running from dark
blue through cyan, yellow and red to dark red.

funcs.name: NewColormap
funcs.doc:
NewColormap builds a Colormap from two or more anchor colors. The anchors are
treated as being equally spaced across [0, 1], and Colormap.At interpolates
linearly between neighbouring anchors. Fewer than two colors are padded so
the result is always usable; the input slice is copied.

funcs.name: Plasma
funcs.doc:
Plasma returns an approximation of matplotlib's "plasma" colormap, running
from dark blue through magenta and orange to yellow.

funcs.name: Viridis
funcs.doc:
Viridis returns matplotlib's default perceptually uniform colormap,
approximated by five anchor colors from dark purple through teal and green to
yellow.

methods.name: At
methods.doc:
At returns the color at position t. Values of t are clamped to [0, 1], so
t <= 0 yields the first anchor and t >= 1 yields the last. Interpolation is
performed independently per channel (including alpha) and rounded to the
nearest 8-bit value.

methods.name: Reversed
methods.doc:
Reversed returns a new Colormap whose anchor colors are in the opposite
order, equivalent to appending "_r" to a matplotlib colormap name.

types.name: ErrorBar
types.doc:
ErrorBar draws markers at (x, y) with symmetric vertical error bars of
half-height yerr[i] and horizontal caps, connected by a line, mirroring
matplotlib's Axes.errorbar.

methods.name: SetColor
methods.doc: SetColor sets the series color and returns the plot for chaining.

methods.name: SetLabel
methods.doc: SetLabel sets the legend label and returns the plot for chaining.

types.name: Figure
types.doc:
Figure is the top-level container for one or more Axes. It owns the output
dimensions (in pixels), background color and the color cycle shared by the
series it contains.

funcs.name: FigSize
funcs.doc:
FigSize starts a fresh current figure with the given pixel dimensions,
discarding any previous global state.

funcs.name: Gcf
funcs.doc: Gcf returns the current figure, creating a default 640x480 one if needed.

funcs.name: NewFigure
funcs.doc:
NewFigure creates a figure of the given pixel size with a white background
and 100 DPI. A width or height less than 1 is clamped to a small default.

methods.name: AddAxes
methods.doc: AddAxes appends a new Axes filling the entire figure and returns it.

methods.name: Axes
methods.doc:
Axes returns the figure's first Axes, creating one if none exist. This is a
convenience for the common single-plot case.

methods.name: PNGBytes
methods.doc: PNGBytes returns the figure encoded as a PNG byte slice.

methods.name: RenderImage
methods.doc: RenderImage renders the figure to an in-memory RGBA image.

methods.name: RenderSVG
methods.doc: RenderSVG renders the figure to an SVG document string.

methods.name: Save
methods.doc:
Save renders the figure to path, choosing the format from the file
extension (".svg" for SVG, anything else for PNG).

methods.name: SavePNG
methods.doc: SavePNG renders and writes the figure to a PNG file at path.

methods.name: SaveSVG
methods.doc: SaveSVG renders and writes the figure to an SVG file at path.

methods.name: Subplots
methods.doc:
Subplots creates a rows x cols grid of Axes and returns them in row-major
order. Each Axes occupies its cell of the figure.

methods.name: WritePNG
methods.doc: WritePNG encodes the figure as PNG to w.

methods.name: WriteSVG
methods.doc: WriteSVG writes the figure's SVG document to w.

types.name: FillBetween
types.doc:
FillBetween shades the region between two curves y1 and y2 sampled at the
same x positions, the equivalent of matplotlib's Axes.fill_between. When Y2
is nil the region is filled down to zero.

methods.name: SetColor
methods.doc:
SetColor sets the fill color and returns the area for chaining. Use
Color.WithAlpha to make the fill translucent.

methods.name: SetLabel
methods.doc: SetLabel sets the legend label and returns the area for chaining.

types.name: Histogram
types.doc: Histogram bins raw data into equal-width bins and draws the counts as bars.

funcs.name: Hist
funcs.doc: Hist adds a histogram to the current axes.

methods.name: SetColor
methods.doc: SetColor sets the histogram color and returns it for chaining.

methods.name: SetLabel
methods.doc: SetLabel sets the legend label and returns the histogram for chaining.

types.name: LinePlot
types.signature:
type LinePlot struct {
	X, Y      []float64
	Color     Color
	LineWidth float64
	Marker    Marker
	Label     string
}
types.doc: LinePlot is a connected line series with optional markers.

funcs.name: Plot
funcs.doc: Plot adds a line series to the current axes.

methods.name: SetColor
methods.doc: SetColor sets the series color and returns the plot for chaining.

methods.name: SetLabel
methods.doc: SetLabel sets the legend label and returns the plot for chaining.

methods.name: SetLineWidth
methods.doc: SetLineWidth sets the line width in pixels and returns the plot for chaining.

methods.name: SetMarker
methods.doc: SetMarker sets the point marker and returns the plot for chaining.

types.name: LogNorm
types.doc:
LogNorm maps positive data values onto the unit interval [0, 1] on a
logarithmic (base-10) scale, the counterpart of matplotlib's colors.LogNorm.
It is typically paired with a Colormap to color data that spans several
orders of magnitude. Both Min and Max must be strictly positive.

funcs.name: NewLogNorm
funcs.doc:
NewLogNorm returns a LogNorm spanning [min, max]. Both bounds should be
strictly positive; a non-positive or degenerate range makes Norm return 0.

methods.name: Map
methods.signature: func (n LogNorm) Map(cm Colormap, v float64) Color
methods.doc:
Map normalizes v with n and looks the result up in cm, returning the color a
logarithmic scalar-mappable would assign to v.

methods.name: Norm
methods.doc:
Norm maps v to [0, 1] by the transform
(log10(v)-log10(Min))/(log10(Max)-log10(Min)), clamped to the unit interval.
Non-positive v, or a degenerate/invalid range, returns 0. This reproduces
matplotlib.colors.LogNorm for in-range values.

types.name: Marker
types.signature: type Marker int
types.doc: Marker identifies the glyph drawn at data points for line and scatter plots.

types.name: Normalize
types.doc:
Normalize maps data values in a fixed range onto the unit interval [0, 1],
the counterpart of matplotlib's colors.Normalize. It is typically paired with
a Colormap to color scalar data.

funcs.name: NewNormalize
funcs.doc:
NewNormalize returns a Normalize spanning [min, max]. If min == max the range
is treated as degenerate and Norm always returns 0.

methods.name: Map
methods.signature: func (n Normalize) Map(cm Colormap, v float64) Color
methods.doc:
Map normalizes v with n and looks the result up in cm, returning the color
that a scalar-mappable would assign to v.

methods.name: Norm
methods.doc:
Norm maps v to [0, 1] by the linear transform (v-Min)/(Max-Min), clamped to
the unit interval. A degenerate range (Min == Max) returns 0.

types.name: PieChart
types.doc:
PieChart draws proportional wedges. It does not use the axis coordinate
system; the enclosing axes renders it specially.

funcs.name: Pie
funcs.doc: Pie adds a pie chart to the current axes.

types.name: Point
types.doc:
Point is a 2-D coordinate in device (pixel) space, with the origin at the
top-left corner and y increasing downward.

types.name: RefLine
types.signature:
type RefLine struct {
	Value     float64
	Vertical  bool
	Color     Color
	LineWidth float64
	Label     string
}
types.doc:
RefLine is a full-width horizontal or full-height vertical reference line at
a fixed data coordinate, the equivalent of matplotlib's axhline and axvline.
A reference line does not participate in autoscaling.

methods.name: SetColor
methods.doc: SetColor sets the line color and returns the reference line for chaining.

methods.name: SetLabel
methods.doc: SetLabel sets the legend label and returns the reference line for chaining.

types.name: ScatterPlot
types.signature:
type ScatterPlot struct {
	X, Y   []float64
	Color  Color
	Size   float64
	Marker Marker
	Label  string
}
types.doc: ScatterPlot is a set of unconnected markers.

funcs.name: Scatter
funcs.doc: Scatter adds a scatter series to the current axes.

methods.name: SetColor
methods.doc: SetColor sets the marker color and returns the plot for chaining.

methods.name: SetLabel
methods.doc: SetLabel sets the legend label and returns the plot for chaining.

methods.name: SetMarker
methods.doc: SetMarker sets the marker glyph and returns the plot for chaining.

methods.name: SetSize
methods.doc: SetSize sets the marker size in pixels and returns the plot for chaining.

types.name: StemPlot
types.doc:
StemPlot draws a vertical stem from a baseline at y = 0 up to each data
point, with a marker at the tip, mirroring matplotlib's Axes.stem.

methods.name: SetColor
methods.doc: SetColor sets the stem color and returns the plot for chaining.

methods.name: SetLabel
methods.doc: SetLabel sets the legend label and returns the plot for chaining.

types.name: StepPlot
types.signature:
type StepPlot struct {
	X, Y      []float64
	Color     Color
	LineWidth float64
	Where     string
	Label     string
}
types.doc:
StepPlot is a piecewise-constant ("staircase") line, the equivalent of
matplotlib's Axes.step. The Where field selects where the step transition
occurs relative to the data points: "pre", "post" (the default) or "mid".

methods.name: SetColor
methods.doc: SetColor sets the step color and returns the plot for chaining.

methods.name: SetLabel
methods.doc: SetLabel sets the legend label and returns the plot for chaining.

methods.name: SetWhere
methods.doc:
SetWhere sets the step placement ("pre", "post" or "mid") and returns the
plot for chaining. Unrecognized values are treated as "post".

types.name: TextAnchor
types.signature: type TextAnchor int
types.doc:
TextAnchor controls horizontal alignment of rendered text relative to its
anchor point.

funcs.name: Arange
funcs.doc:
Arange returns evenly spaced values over the half-open interval [start, stop)
using the given step, mirroring numpy.arange. A positive step walks upward
until reaching or passing stop; a negative step walks downward. A zero step
(or a step whose sign cannot reach stop) yields an empty slice.

funcs.name: Clf
funcs.doc: Clf clears the current figure, resetting the global state.

funcs.name: Grid
funcs.doc: Grid toggles the grid on the current axes.

funcs.name: HSVToRGB
funcs.signature: func HSVToRGB(h, s, v float64) (r, g, b float64)
funcs.doc:
HSVToRGB converts an HSV color, with each component given as a float in
[0, 1], back to RGB. The returned red, green and blue are each in [0, 1]. It
mirrors matplotlib.colors.hsv_to_rgb and is the inverse of RGBToHSV, so
round-tripping a color through both reproduces the original channels.

funcs.name: Legend
funcs.doc: Legend enables the legend on the current axes.

funcs.name: Linspace
funcs.signature: func Linspace(start, stop float64, num int) []float64
funcs.doc:
Linspace returns num evenly spaced values covering the closed interval
[start, stop]. It mirrors numpy.linspace, which matplotlib users routinely
reach for when constructing plot coordinates.

The endpoints are always included exactly: the first element equals start
and, when num > 1, the last element equals stop. If num <= 0 an empty slice
is returned; if num == 1 the single-element slice {start} is returned.

funcs.name: Logspace
funcs.signature: func Logspace(start, stop float64, num int, base float64) []float64
funcs.doc:
Logspace returns num values spaced evenly on a logarithmic scale, mirroring
numpy.logspace. The returned values are base raised to the exponents
Linspace(start, stop, num); that is, the exponents (not the values
themselves) are linearly spaced. A base <= 0 is treated as 10.

funcs.name: RGBToHSV
funcs.signature: func RGBToHSV(r, g, b float64) (h, s, v float64)
funcs.doc:
RGBToHSV converts an RGB color, with each component given as a float in
[0, 1], to the HSV color space. The returned hue, saturation and value are
each in [0, 1] (hue is the fraction of a full turn, so 1/3 is green and 2/3
is blue). It mirrors matplotlib.colors.rgb_to_hsv exactly, including its
convention that a fully unsaturated color reports a hue of 0.

funcs.name: Save
funcs.doc: Save renders the current figure to path (format chosen by extension).

funcs.name: Title
funcs.doc: Title sets the current axes title.

funcs.name: Xlabel
funcs.doc: Xlabel sets the current axes x-axis label.

funcs.name: Xlim
funcs.doc: Xlim fixes the current axes x-limits.

funcs.name: Ylabel
funcs.doc: Ylabel sets the current axes y-axis label.

funcs.name: Ylim
funcs.doc: Ylim fixes the current axes y-limits.

examples.code:
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"strings"

	"github.com/malcolmston/matplotlib"
)

func main() {
	fig := matplotlib.NewFigure(320, 240)
	ax := fig.AddAxes()
	ax.Plot([]float64{0, 1, 2, 3}, []float64{0, 1, 4, 9}).SetLabel("x^2")
	ax.SetTitle("Squares").SetXLabel("x").SetYLabel("y").Legend()

	svg := fig.RenderSVG()
	fmt.Printf("size: %dx%d\n", fig.Width, fig.Height)
	fmt.Println("has polyline:", strings.Contains(svg, "<polyline"))
	fmt.Println("has title:", strings.Contains(svg, "Squares"))
}
examples.output:
size: 320x240
has polyline: true
has title: true
examples.doc:
Example renders a tiny line plot to SVG and prints its dimensions and a
snippet of the output, demonstrating the basic Figure/Axes workflow.

examples.name: Figure (Save)
examples.doc:
ExampleFigure_Save writes a bar chart to a PNG file using the pyplot-style
convenience API.
